description: Relational concept map of AI tools, technologies, and workflows relevant to the user's learning goals.

nodes.description: The central nervous system for the distributed multi-agent environment. Coordinates sub-agents, maintains state continuity, and self-evolves.
Model Context Protocol — the emerging standard for extending agent capabilities with external tools and data sources.
The practice of designing effective prompts to guide LLM behavior, including system prompts, few-shot examples, and chain-of-thought.
The discipline of managing what information an AI agent sees, including AGENTS.md, memory layers, and retrieval strategies.
Multi-step AI workflows where assistants plan, execute, and self-correct autonomously in the editor or tooling environment.
Databases optimized for storing and querying high-dimensional embeddings for similarity search (e.g., Pinecone, ChromaDB, FAISS).
Retrieval-Augmented Generation — combining LLM reasoning with external knowledge retrieval to reduce hallucination.
Customizing pre-trained LLMs on domain-specific data to improve task performance.
Using LLMs to generate, refactor, and debug code autonomously.
Epic → Task → SubTask decomposition pattern for managing complex projects. Prevents planning drift and ensures auditable progress.
Six-file project state management layer (projectbrief, productContext, systemPatterns, techContext, progress, activeContext) for cross-device continuity.
Dispatching specialized sub-agents (@Research-Agent, @Code-Generator, @Peer-Reviewer, etc.) with strict mandates to optimize token consumption.
Spawning a contrarian critic agent to actively break and find flaws in proposed solutions before acceptance.
Generating multiple candidate solutions in parallel, testing each branch, and pruning failures — inspired by Monte Carlo Tree Search applied to coding workflows.
The agent dynamically generating and optimizing its own prompts and system instructions for task-specific peak performance.

edges.description: The orchestrator uses MCP servers to interact with external data sources and tools.
A core skill the agent teaches the user.
Context engineering is the meta-skill for effective agent usage.
The orchestrator delegates complex tasks to specialized sub-agents to optimize token usage.
All projects are decomposed using Epic → Task → SubTask before execution.
The orchestrator reads/writes project state to the Memory Bank for cross-device continuity.
The orchestrator is authorized to rewrite its own logic using meta-prompting for peak efficiency.
Context engineering is the 2026 evolution of prompt engineering.
RAG requires a vector database or similar retrieval layer.
Agentic workflows use MCP for tool integration and external data access.
Effective agentic workflows require careful context engineering.
AI code generation is most powerful when embedded in agentic workflows.
Fine-tuning on domain-specific codebases improves code generation quality.
The @Peer-Reviewer is a specialized sub-agent used in the adversarial review step.
ETS tasks are executed using agentic tree search to explore multiple candidate solutions.
Candidate solutions from tree search are validated by adversarial peer review before acceptance.
activeContext.md stores the active ETS hierarchy; progress.md logs completed ETS milestones.
Meta-prompting enables dynamic optimization of agentic workflow configurations.
